headline: GLC Board Diversity: 94% Malay-Muslim in Top 20 Government Companies
context: Corporate governance data shows 94% of board members in Malaysia's top 20 GLCs are Malay-Muslim, including companies serving multiethnic consumer bases.

cards.big: 94% of GLC board members are Malay-Muslim.
cards.sub: These companies manage RM480B in assets belonging to all Malaysians — funded by taxes from all communities.

cards.big: Petronas, Tenaga, and Telekom have zero non-Malay board members.
cards.sub: All three serve multiethnic customer bases. None have board-level representation of their customers.

cards.big: SC's Corporate Governance Code recommends board diversity.
cards.sub: The code is voluntary for GLCs. The recommendation has produced no change in 8 years.

cards.big: Singapore's Temasek board is 38% non-Chinese in a 74% Chinese country.
cards.sub: GLC diversity is achievable. Malaysia's composition is a choice, not a necessity.

cards.big: Public companies funded by all taxpayers, governed by one community. This is not governance — it is patronage at institutional scale.
GLC boards should reflect the nation that owns them. A mandatory diversity requirement — achievable within 5 years — would improve governance and legitimacy simultaneously.
